trade.summary: Saudi Arabia-Taiwan trade is commercially significant. Saudi Arabia exports crude oil to Taiwan's refineries (Formosa Petrochemical, CPC Corporation) — Taiwan is a major energy importer wholly dependent on external sources. Taiwan exports semiconductors, electronics, petrochemical equipment, and machinery to Saudi Arabia. Saudi Aramco and Taiwanese petrochemical companies have long-standing supply relationships. TSMC components increasingly find their way into Saudi industrial applications.

diplomatic.status: informal — Saudi Arabia follows One China Policy, no formal recognition of Taiwan
diplomatic.summary: Saudi Arabia does not formally recognize Taiwan, adhering to the One China Policy consistent with its relationship with mainland China. Relations are conducted through trade offices. Saudi Arabia has been deepening ties with China (including a strategic partnership with Beijing) which constrains its relationship with Taiwan. However, economic pragmatism drives substantial Taiwan-Saudi interaction at the commercial level. Saudi Arabia's focus on investment returns means Taiwan's semiconductor sector is recognized as strategically important.
recent_incidents: Saudi Arabia and China signed comprehensive strategic partnership (2022) — constrains Saudi-Taiwan relationship
Taiwan's semiconductor supply to Saudi Arabia's tech projects is commercially important despite political limitations

military.summary: No military relationship. Saudi Arabia's primary security relationship is with the United States. Taiwan is not part of any Middle Eastern security framework. No military contacts.

financial.summary: Saudi Arabia's PIF has made investments in global tech companies that may include Taiwanese firms. Taiwan's semiconductor supply chains are critical for Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 tech ambitions (NEOM smart city, tech sector development). Some Taiwanese tech companies have expressed interest in establishing a presence in Saudi Arabia's tech hubs.

energy.summary: Saudi Arabia is one of Taiwan's most important crude oil suppliers. Taiwan imports approximately 10-15% of its crude from Saudi Arabia. CPC Corporation and Saudi Aramco have long-term supply agreements. The energy relationship is strategically important for Taiwan's energy security given its lack of domestic energy resources.

scientific.summary: Limited but growing scientific collaboration. Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 tech ambitions create demand for Taiwanese semiconductor and electronics expertise. KAUST has engaged with some Taiwanese researchers. The semiconductor technology transfer dimension is constrained by geopolitical considerations.
